title: Graaff-Reinet matriculant shines with eight distinctions
description: GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - The community is proud...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - The community is proud to celebrate the outstanding matric achievements of Emma Short, who achieved an exceptional eight distinctions in the 2025 IEB matric examinations.","html":"GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - The community is proud to celebrate the outstanding matric achievements of Emma Short, who achieved an exceptional eight distinctions in the 2025 IEB matric examinations."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Her distinctions were in the subjects accounting, Afrikaans first additional language, English home language, history, life orientation, life sciences, mathematics and further studies mathematics standard (calculus and algebra)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Emma is the daughter of David and Kirsten Short. She began her schooling at Union High School before heading to Makhanda in Grade 8 to attend the Diocesan School for Girls (DSG), continuing a proud family legacy.","html":"Emma is the daughter of David and Kirsten Short. She began her schooling at Union High School before heading to Makhanda in Grade 8 to attend the Diocesan School for Girls (DSG), continuing a proud family legacy."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Her father, David (OA ’91), uncle Lloyd (OA ’93) and grandfather Arthur (OA ’64) all attended St Andrew’s College, sister school to DSG.","html":"Her father, David (OA ’91), uncle Lloyd (OA ’93) and grandfather Arthur (OA ’64) all attended St Andrew’s College, sister school to DSG."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Beyond academics, Emma was an enthusiastic participant in school life, excelling in both dance and tennis.","html":"Beyond academics, Emma was an enthusiastic participant in school life, excelling in both dance and tennis."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"She represented DSG in the first tennis team in 2024 and 2025, received half-colours for dance in 2025, and contributed meaningfully to the school community as a member of the Sacristans.","html":"She represented DSG in the first tennis team in 2024 and 2025, received half-colours for dance in 2025, and contributed meaningfully to the school community as a member of the Sacristans."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Emma’s achievements reflect her dedication, versatility and commitment, and she is a source of great pride to her family and the wider Graaff-Reinet.","html":"Emma’s achievements reflect her dedication, versatility and commitment, and she is a source of great pride to her family and the wider Graaff-Reinet."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Graaff-Reinet matriculant shines with eight distinctions
